title: NQ Europe Offer Reversal Into Upper Liquidity
focus_questions: Did lower support appear before the upward release?
Was upper liquidity actually harvested or only tagged once?

summary.thesis: A prior upper sell band can flip from suppression into target when lower support responds and aggressive buying lifts price into the band.
summary.operator_translation: Do not treat this like a random breakout; the reversal must be built from below first.
summary.ai_usage_note: Prefer this card when the replay shows suppression above, support below, and real upward release into the same overhead band.
summary.ai_brief: Environment: Europe-session suppression that begins failing from below. Required evidence: lower support response, upward initiative, and challenge of the same upper band. Do not trade: if support never appears or the move is only a weak bounce. Real trigger: aggressive reclaim from the lower zone into upper liquidity. Failure point: the upper band stays intact and price cannot accept upward.

when_relevant: A visible upper sell band first acts as suppression.
Lower support or response begins forming before the reversal.
Aggressive buying lifts price back into the prior upper band.
required_evidence: significant_liquidity is visible above price before the reversal
lower support response is visible before the upward release
initiative_drive shifts upward with real travel into the upper band
confirmation_signals: lower support forms before the lift begins
aggressive buy flow reclaims price back toward the upper band
the upper band is consumed, pulled, or accepted through instead of only tagged
invalidation_signals: lower support never forms or quickly disappears
upward release stalls before challenging the upper band
the upper band keeps rejecting price without consumption or acceptance
no_trade_conditions: Do not buy only because the upper wall exists; the lower support response must appear first.
Do not buy a random bounce in the middle of a range with no suppression-to-release sequence.
Do not keep buying if the upper band remains intact and price cannot show real upward acceptance.

entry_archetypes.name: support-led reclaim
entry_archetypes.description: Long after the lower response zone produces a real upward reclaim into the prior suppression band.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the reclaim still occurs under intact overhead pressure with no lift quality.

entry_archetypes.name: acceptance through upper band
entry_archetypes.description: Long after price begins accepting through the band that previously capped the market.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the move is already extended and post-harvest failure risk is high.

management_notes: Track whether the upper band is consumed or only tested.
If the move completes the upper objective, switch attention to post-harvest behavior.
If the lower support source fails, the reversal thesis weakens quickly.
review_questions: What evidence proved lower support was real before the reversal was taken?
Did the move truly harvest upper liquidity, or only squeeze into it briefly?
Was the long entry aligned with the broader session script or fighting stronger overhead context?
